A_DRIVE.definition: Production arcade cabinet drive containing LaunchBox, ROMs, and BIOS files
A_DRIVE.detection: Checks for paths starting with 'A:' or '/MNT/A' (WSL)

AA_DRIVE_ROOT.definition: Environment variable pointing to Arcade Assistant root directory
AA_DRIVE_ROOT.example: A:\ for production, relative path for development

LAUNCHBOX_ROOT.definition: Optional environment variable to override LaunchBox location

FASTAPI_URL.definition: Backend service URL used by gateway

mock_data.definition: 15 sample games loaded when A: drive is not available

platform_xml.definition: XML files containing game metadata for each platform

sanctioned_paths.definition: Directories where backend is allowed to write files

lazy_loading.definition: Parser initializes on first API request rather than startup
lazy_loading.benefit: Faster server startup, deferred XML parsing

parser_cache.definition: JSON file storing parsed game data to avoid re-parsing XMLs

plugin_bridge.definition: C# plugin running in LaunchBox for native game launching
plugin_bridge.fallback: Uses LaunchBox.exe or direct MAME if unavailable

lifespan.definition: FastAPI async context manager for startup/shutdown events
lifespan.purpose: Initialize cache, validate environment, cleanup on exit

uvicorn.definition: ASGI server running the FastAPI application
uvicorn.modes: reload=True for development, False for production

image_scanner.definition: Service that discovers and caches game image paths

launch_fallback_chain.definition: Sequence of methods tried when launching a game
launch_fallback_chain.reason: CLI_Launcher.exe not found at expected location

error_codes.404: Resource not found (game, endpoint, or file)
